fundamentals.name: Bloom Energy Corporation
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Bloom Energy Corporation designs, manufactures, sells, and installs solid oxide fuel cell systems for on-site power generation in the United States and internationally. It offers Bloom Energy Server, an energy server platform to convert fuel, such as natural gas, biogas, hydrogen, or a blend of these fuels, into electricity through a non-combustion electrochemical process. The company also provides Bloom Electrolyzer for producing hydrogen. It sells its products through direct and indirect sales channels to utilities, data centers, retail, healthcare, education, telecom, manufacturing, and other industries. The company was formerly known as Ion America Corp. and changed its name to Bloom Energy Corporation in 2006. Bloom Energy Corporation was incorporated in 2001 and is headquartered in San Jose, California.

judgment.summary: Rapidly growing revenue business with significant analyst optimism, but high valuation multiples and leverage present risks.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat estimates by 242.4%; next earnings date is 2026-04-28.
positives: Revenue growth 130.4% YoY is exceptionally strong.
Analyst growth estimates are very high for the current quarter (286.1%) and fiscal year (179.8%).
Last quarter's EPS beat estimates by a substantial 242.4%.
Strong current and quick ratios (5.03 and 3.98) indicate good short-term liquidity.
Free cash flow is positive at $265.52M TTM.
negatives: Forward P/E of 69.98 and Price/Sales of 35.13 are very high, suggesting lofty expectations.
Debt/Equity ratio of 311.48 indicates significant leverage.
Profit margin is extremely thin at 0.2%, and Return on Equity is low at 1.3%.
Mean analyst target of $260.18 is below the current stock price.

news.paragraph: Bloom Energy (BE) received an upgrade to Outperform from Daiwa, following recent positive mentions in the press regarding its industry performance and significant deals. The company's stock has notably outperformed its sector over the past six months, and a recent 10-year deal with Nebius was highlighted as potentially altering the bull case. The upcoming earnings report on April 28th will be a key event to watch, especially given the strong analyst growth estimates for the current quarter and fiscal year.
